best_overall_lp.note: Marginal LP lead over DELTA (0.5005 vs 0.4992), within noise

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: DELTA-Matched improves from 2p to 3p while other models degrade
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: MRR 0.733->0.738 (2p->3p), only model with positive delta among 7 tested

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-hop advantage is structural, not a hyperparameter accident
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: DELTA leads GraphGPS on 3p at all 5 DropEdge rates (0-40%)

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: DELTA's compositional advantage grows with reasoning depth
Multi-seed validation confirms DELTA advantage on 3p is statistically robust
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: 3-seed: DELTA 0.742+/-0.009 vs GraphGPS 0.713+/-0.007. Std bars don't overlap

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: DELTA inference cost is comparable to GraphGPS despite 34x training cost
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: Encoding 51.8x slower but per-query scoring 0.8-0.9x GraphGPS

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Learnable temperature keeps sharp attention (all heads retain temp > 2.0)
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: 16/16 heads (delta_matched) and 24/24 heads (delta_full) maintain temp > 2.0 at convergence

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Temperature sharpening activates DELTA-Full's excess capacity on 3p
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: DELTA-Full temp=4.0: 3p MRR 0.4018 vs temp=1.0: 0.3725, +0.029. Dead heads 83%->38%.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Edge temps drift UP, node temps drift DOWN — edge attention benefits from sharpness, node attention prefers averaging
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: delta_full temp=4.0: L2_edge 4.029->4.524, L2_node 3.992->3.587. Consistent pattern across all layers.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Layer-specific temperature (L0 soft, L1+L2 sharp) achieves best LP MRR while reducing dead heads
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: B_layer_sharp: LP MRR=0.4783 (best of 4 conditions), dead=9/24 (38%), 3p MRR=0.3908 (+0.018 vs A)

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Node attention requires temperature sharpening to activate — edge-only sharpening insufficient
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: C_edge_sharp: L1 node heads 100% dead, L2 node 50-100% dead despite edge temps at 4.0. B with node temp=4.0: L1+L2 node heads 0% dead.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Asymmetric node/edge temperature (following drift direction) improves LP MRR over symmetric sharpening
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: E (node=2,edge=6): LP MRR=0.4856 (+1.5% over B's 0.4783). F (node=3,edge=5): LP MRR=0.4837. Both beat B on LP.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Node temperatures stay stable at initialization while edge temps always drift UP
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: E: node 2.0→2.01, edge L2 6.0→6.68. F: node 3.0→3.01, edge L2 5.0→5.60. G: node 2.5→2.50, edge L2 5.0→5.60. Consistent across all conditions.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: L0=(4,4) + E's asymmetric L1+L2 achieves new LP MRR record
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: H: LP MRR=0.4887 (+0.003 over E's 0.4856). J: LP MRR=0.4872. Both pass LP target.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: L0 node temperature is irrelevant — L0 is 100% dead and gradient-free
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: H (L0_node init=4.0, final=4.039) and J (L0_node init=2.0, final=2.048) produce near-identical L1+L2 temps and performance (LP 0.4887 vs 0.4872, 3p 0.3930 vs 0.3911). L0 node stays near init because gradient is zero through dead heads.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Temperature annealing (node 4.0→2.0 fast) breaks the 3p ceiling
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: K_anneal_fast: 3p MRR=0.4148 — FIRST configuration to beat D's 0.4018 (+0.013) across 5 phases of temperature experiments. Best checkpoint at ep 175 with node temp=2.6.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Early training with high node temp builds 3p-supportive representations that persist after annealing
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: K (anneal 4→2 fast) achieves 3p=0.4148 while H (static node=2.0) only 3p=0.3930. Both end with similar node temps (~2.0-2.6), but K trained early epochs at node=4.0. The trajectory matters, not just final temp.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Training trajectory (annealing) provides +0.015 3p bonus over static initialization at same final temperature
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: K (annealed to 2.6 at checkpoint) achieves 3p=0.4148 vs N (static 2.6) 3p=0.4001 — delta=+0.0147. The trajectory through high node temps during early training creates 3p-supportive representations that static init cannot replicate.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Moderate annealing (4→2.5) achieves new LP MRR record
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: P_anneal_moderate: LP MRR=0.4890 (+0.0003 over H/M's 0.4887), H@10=0.8014 (new H@10 record). Best checkpoint at ep 200 with node=2.800.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Edge init 7.0 (vs 6.0) boosts LP MRR via sharper edge attention
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: Q (edge=7.0): LP=0.4905 vs K (edge=6.0): LP=0.4819 — delta=+0.0086. S (edge=7.0+faster): LP=0.4902 vs R (edge=6.0+faster): LP=0.4793 — delta=+0.0109. Edge sharpness consistently boosts LP.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Temperature landscape fully characterized — three distinct operating modes exist
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: After 7 phases (46-52), 20+ configs: LP-optimized (P/Q, LP≥0.4890), Balanced-3p (K, 3p=0.4148), Deep-reasoning (N, 4p=0.3426, 5p=0.3788). No single config achieves combined LP+3p target. Temperature controls reasoning depth.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: LP MRR improvements from temperature tuning are statistically robust across seeds
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: K: 0.4832±0.0052 (all 3 seeds > 0.476). N: 0.4842±0.0089 (all 3 seeds > 0.474). LP improvements from asymmetric temperature are real and reproducible.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: 10k-query evaluation reduces cross-seed multi-hop variance by ≥50%
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: Average reduction: K=75.0%, N=79.1%. Range 66.9-84.1%. Evaluation noise was the dominant source of P53's variance, not model noise.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: With tight CIs at 10k queries, temperature-tuned DELTA-Full multi-hop performance is indistinguishable between K and N
BrainEncoder constructed edges deliver H@10 improvement over delta_full baseline
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: brain_hybrid H@10=0.7973 vs delta_full H@10=0.7603, delta=+0.037. 4870 constructed edges at 2% density improve top-10 recall by 4.9%. Architecture viable.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Lower constructor density (0.01) outperforms higher density (0.02) on brain_hybrid LP
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: C (d=0.01): MRR=0.4794, H@10=0.8076, 2435 edges vs D (d=0.02): MRR=0.4678, H@10=0.7500, 4870 edges. Half the edges yields +0.012 MRR and +0.058 H@10. Fewer, higher-quality edges strictly dominate.

confirmed_hypotheses.hypothesis: brain_hybrid @ density=0.01 matches delta_full baseline MRR while preserving H@10 advantage
confirmed_hypotheses.evidence: C MRR=0.4794 vs delta_full MRR=0.4796 (delta=-0.0002, within noise). C H@10=0.8076 vs delta_full H@10=0.7603 (+4.7%). Constructed edges add recall without precision penalty at optimal density.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Generalization gap between val and test MRR indicates overfitting
failed_hypotheses.evidence: Weight decay sweep showed no pattern -- gap is val-set noise

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: DropEdge regularization improves multi-hop performance
failed_hypotheses.evidence: Marginal effect for both models. Reframed as robustness check.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Temperature reduces dead heads below 50% for delta_matched
failed_hypotheses.evidence: 69%->50%, missed <50% threshold by 1 head. PARTIAL for matched, CONFIRMED for full (83%->38%)

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Layer-specific or edge-only sharpening matches D's 3p improvement
failed_hypotheses.evidence: B: 3p=0.3908 (vs D 0.4018, gap=0.011). C: 3p=0.3678 (worse than A). Neither matched D's full 3p benefit.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Asymmetric node/edge temp achieves both B's LP (≥0.4783) and D's 3p (≥0.4018)
failed_hypotheses.evidence: E LP=0.4856 (PASS) but 3p=0.3872 (FAIL). G: closest 3p=0.3970 but LP=0.4699 (FAIL). LP/3p trade-off persists.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: L0 temperature at 4.0 explains D's 3p advantage (≥0.4018)
failed_hypotheses.evidence: H (L0=4,4 + E's L1+L2): 3p=0.3930 (FAIL). I (L0=4,4 + F's L1+L2): 3p=0.3783 (FAIL). J (L0=2,4 + E's L1+L2): 3p=0.3911 (FAIL). None matched D's 3p=0.4018. LP/3p trade-off is fundamental to asymmetric temperature init.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined LP+3p target met via annealing (LP≥0.4856 AND 3p≥0.4018)
failed_hypotheses.evidence: K achieves 3p=0.4148 (PASS) but LP=0.4819 (FAIL, gap=-0.004). M achieves LP=0.4887 (PASS) but 3p=0.3803 (FAIL). Pareto frontier shifted but combined target not met.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Slow annealing (4→2 over full training) beats fast annealing
failed_hypotheses.evidence: L_anneal_slow: LP=0.4803, 3p=0.3775 — REJECTED on both metrics. Fast annealing (K) strictly dominates slow (L).

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Static node=2.6 matches K's 3p (≥0.4018)
failed_hypotheses.evidence: N_static_2.6: 3p=0.4001, misses target by 0.002. LP=0.4746 (FAIL). Static init at K's optimal checkpoint value cannot replicate K's annealed 3p=0.4148.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Static node=3.2 (L/M checkpoint value) achieves 3p≥0.4018
failed_hypotheses.evidence: O_static_3.2: 3p=0.3764, LP=0.4785 — REJECTED on both metrics. Higher static node temp is worse for 3p than lower (N's 0.4001).

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined LP+3p target met via moderate annealing (P: 4→2.5)
failed_hypotheses.evidence: P: LP=0.4890 (PASS, new record) but 3p=0.3823 (FAIL). Moderate anneal endpoint (2.5 vs K's 2.0) preserves LP but loses K's 3p advantage.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Q (K+edge7) achieves combined LP≥0.4856 AND 3p≥0.4018
failed_hypotheses.evidence: Q: LP=0.4905 (PASS, new record) but 3p=0.3927 (FAIL, -0.009 vs target). Edge sharpness boosts LP at the expense of 3p.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Faster annealing (35% vs 50%) closes K's LP gap
failed_hypotheses.evidence: R (35% schedule): LP=0.4793 (FAIL, worse than K's 0.4819). Faster anneal loses LP without significant 3p gain (0.4114 vs K's 0.4148).

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined edge7+faster annealing (S) achieves both targets
failed_hypotheses.evidence: S: LP=0.4902 (PASS), H@10=0.8045 (new record!), but 3p=0.3789 (WORST of all 3 P52 conditions). Edge sharpness + speed = LP-biased.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: K's 3p advantage (≥0.4018) is statistically robust across 3 seeds
failed_hypotheses.evidence: K mean 3p=0.3699±0.0200, min=0.3418 — BELOW baseline A (0.3725). K's 3p=0.4148 from Phase 50 was a single-seed outlier. Not reproducible.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: N's deep-hop advantage (4p≥0.30, 5p≥0.30) is statistically robust
failed_hypotheses.evidence: N mean 4p=0.2354±0.0618, mean 5p=0.2665±0.0738. min 4p=0.1912, min 5p=0.2012. HUGE variance, no seed passes both targets. N's single-seed records (4p=0.3426, 5p=0.3788) were outliers.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Seed=42 results are reproducible (CUDA determinism)
failed_hypotheses.evidence: K seed=42 re-run: LP=0.4880 (was 0.4819), 3p=0.3812 (was 0.4148). N seed=42 re-run: LP=0.4744 (was 0.4746), 3p=0.3996 (was 0.4001). CUDA non-determinism causes significant variation even with same seed.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: 10k-query multi-hop values match 500q absolute values (only variance differs)
failed_hypotheses.evidence: K 3p at 500q: 0.3699 vs 10kq: 0.2614 — dramatic drop. The query distribution changes at larger sample sizes, sampling harder paths. Direct comparison across query counts is invalid for absolute MRR.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: BrainEncoder achieves LP MRR >= 0.475 on FB15k-237
failed_hypotheses.evidence: brain_hybrid MRR=0.4773, misses 0.475 threshold by -0.002. Matches historical baseline (+0.003 vs Phase 46 A). H@10=0.7973 (+0.037 vs delta_full) is strong. Architecture viable but needs optimization.

failed_hypotheses.hypothesis: Reducing constructor density from 0.02 to 0.01 improves brain_hybrid LP MRR to >= 0.480
failed_hypotheses.evidence: C (d=0.01): MRR=0.4794, misses 0.480 target by 0.0006. Direction confirmed (C beats D by +0.012 MRR) but absolute target not reached. Single seed, no statistical confidence.

discovered_patterns: delta_matched improves with reasoning depth while all baselines degrade monotonically
smaller DELTA (157K) beats larger DELTA (293K) on compositional reasoning -- capacity paradox
advantage gap doubles at each hop depth: +0.004, +0.026, +0.066, +0.100
DELTA encoding is 51.8x slower but per-query inference is 0.8-0.9x faster than GraphGPS
training cost is dominated by 2-hop edge adjacency computation (34x slower per epoch)
both models peak at ep 125-150, overtrain after -- early stopping critical
DELTA-Matched consistently peaks at ep 125 across all seeds and experiments
GraphGPS benefits more from DropEdge in absolute terms but can't close architectural gap
DELTA attention is mathematically degenerate: d_head=12 + degree~40 -> normalized entropy 0.87 (near-uniform). Model bypasses attention via residual connections.
cross-depth cosine similarity = 1.0 everywhere — encoding is query-independent. Compositional advantage lives in representation structure, not depth-dependent routing.
temperature sharpening helps DELTA-Full 3p (+0.029) but hurts DELTA-Matched 3p (-0.017) — smaller model's residual bypass was already optimal
Layer 0 always 100% dead regardless of temperature — first layer does neighborhood averaging, not selective attention
edge attention benefits from sharpness (temps drift UP), node attention prefers soft averaging (temps drift DOWN) — learned automatically via temperature gradient
DELTA-Full Layer 2 activates with temp=4.0 (0% dead from epoch 100 onward), explaining 3p improvement — 3rd layer unlocked by sharp attention
layer-specific temperature (B: L0=1,L1+L2=4) achieves best LP MRR of all conditions (0.4783) — selective sharpening better than uniform
node attention NEEDS explicit sharpening to activate: C (edge-only sharp) keeps L1 node 100% dead, but B (node+edge sharp at L1+L2) activates all node heads
in B, node temps at L1+L2 drift DOWN from 4.0→3.5-3.7 — optimal node temp likely between 2-3, not 4.0
condition C shows edge temps drift to 4.5 even starting from 4.0 (L0 edge → 4.46, L2 edge → 4.49) — edge attention consistently wants more sharpness
asymmetric node/edge temperature (E: node=2,edge=6 at L1+L2) achieves NEW LP MRR record 0.4856, surpassing B's 0.4783 by +1.5%
node temps are 'set and forget': they stay within 0.01 of initial value across all conditions (E: 2.0→2.01, F: 3.0→3.01, G: 2.5→2.50)
edge temps ALWAYS drift UP, and L2 drifts more than L1: E L1_edge 6.0→6.27 but L2_edge 6.0→6.68. Deeper layers need more edge sharpness.
LP MRR and 3p MRR show persistent trade-off: E best LP (0.4856) has moderate 3p (0.3872); G best 3p (0.3970) has worst LP (0.4699)
D's 3p advantage (0.4018) may come from L0=4.0 — all P48 conditions had L0=1.0 and none matched D's 3p
L0 temperature does NOT explain D's 3p advantage — Phase 49 H (L0=4,4 + E's asymmetric L1+L2) achieves 3p=0.3930, still below D's 0.4018
LP/3p trade-off is fundamental to asymmetric temperature: any init that separates node/edge temps improves LP but cannot match D's uniform 3p
D's 3p advantage (0.4018) is unique to uniform temp=4.0 initialization — neither layer-specific, asymmetric, nor L0-augmented approaches replicate it
L0 node temperature is irrelevant: H (init=4.0) and J (init=2.0) converge to identical L1+L2 temps and near-identical performance because L0 is 100% dead
I (L0=4,4 + F's node=3,edge=5) achieves best-ever H@10 (0.8076) but worst 3p (0.3783) of the three P49 conditions — F's L1+L2 config is LP-biased
Temperature annealing breaks the 3p ceiling: K (node 4→2 fast) achieves 3p=0.4148, the FIRST config to beat D's 0.4018 in 5 phases
K's best checkpoint (ep 175) has node temp=2.6 — not the final anneal target (2.0) — suggesting optimal 3p node temp is ~2.6, not 2.0
All 3 annealing conditions peak at similar epochs (175-200) — early training dynamics are identical regardless of schedule, divergence happens after ep 175
K achieves lowest dead head count: 8/24 (33%) vs 9/24 (38%) for all prior asymmetric configs — annealing activates one additional head
After annealing period ends, K's node temps continue drifting DOWN (2.0→1.93) — model wants lower node temps post-annealing, confirming 'set and forget' pattern
L and M best checkpoints both at ep 200 with node=3.2 despite different schedules — convergent checkpoint selection
K achieves strongest 4p (0.3107) and 5p (0.2811) of any condition tested — annealing benefits ALL multi-hop depths, not just 3p
P_anneal_moderate achieves new LP MRR record (0.4890, +0.0003 over H/M) and new H@10 record (0.8014) — moderate annealing endpoint preserves/improves LP
N_static_2.6 achieves best-ever 4p (0.3426) and 5p (0.3788) — exceeding K's values! Static low node temp benefits deep-hop more than annealing
Trajectory adds +0.015 to 3p: K (annealed to 2.6) 3p=0.4148 vs N (static 2.6) 3p=0.4001. Early high-temp training creates representations static init cannot
Static node temp 3.2 (O) is worse than 2.6 (N) on 3p (0.3764 vs 0.4001) — confirming optimal static node temp is closer to 2.0-2.6, not 3.0+
All Phase 51 conditions peak at ep 200 and early-stop at ep 450 — matching Phase 50 dynamics. Training schedule is robust across temperature configs
K remains closest to combined target: LP gap only −0.004 (0.4819 vs 0.4856) while exceeding 3p target by +0.013 (0.4148 vs 0.4018)
Edge init 7.0 boosts LP by +0.009 (Q vs K: 0.4905 vs 0.4819) but HURTS 3p by −0.022 (Q vs K: 0.3927 vs 0.4148) — edge sharpness and 3p are anti-correlated
Q achieves NEW LP MRR record: 0.4905 (+0.0015 over P's 0.4890). S achieves NEW H@10 record: 0.8045 (+0.003 over P's 0.8014)
Faster annealing (R, 35%) is slightly worse than K (50%) on both LP (0.4793 vs 0.4819) and 3p (0.4114 vs 0.4148) — 50% schedule is optimal
Combined LP+3p target is UNACHIEVABLE in a single temperature config after 7 phases (46-52) and 20+ configurations. The trade-off is fundamental.
Temperature controls reasoning DEPTH: LP-optimized configs (P/Q) use sharper edges, balanced configs (K) use annealing trajectory, deep-reasoning configs (N) use static low node temp
Three operating modes identified: LP-optimized (P/Q, LP≥0.4890), Balanced-3p (K, 3p=0.4148), Deep-reasoning (N, 4p=0.3426/5p=0.3788)
CRITICAL: Multi-hop metrics (3p/4p/5p) at 500-query cap are TOO NOISY for single-seed conclusions. K mean 3p=0.3699±0.0200 (below A baseline!), N 4p std=0.062, 5p std=0.074.
LP MRR IS robust across seeds: K=0.4832±0.0052, N=0.4842±0.0089. Temperature tuning improves LP reliably.
CUDA non-determinism: same seed=42 gives different results across runs. K 3p went from 0.4148 to 0.3812. LP varies by ~0.006.
Multi-hop eval needs MUCH more queries (10k+) or more seeds (5+) to draw reliable conclusions about 3p/4p/5p improvements
Three operating modes narrative is REVOKED for multi-hop — only LP differences are statistically supported
10k-query eval reduces cross-seed multi-hop std by 66-84% — evaluation noise was the dominant variance source, not model noise (CUDA non-determinism)
500q vs 10kq multi-hop MRR gives dramatically different absolute values (K 3p: 0.37→0.26) — generate_extended_queries samples progressively harder paths at larger counts
With tight 10kq CIs, K and N are indistinguishable on multi-hop — temperature does NOT differentially affect multi-hop reasoning in DELTA-Full
Model-to-model multi-hop std at 10kq: 0.003-0.017 — this is the irreducible floor from training stochasticity + CUDA non-determinism
DELTA-Full multi-hop investigation CLOSED (Phases 46-54): temperature reliably improves LP MRR but has no statistically supported effect on multi-hop reasoning depth
open_questions: L0 temperature does NOT explain D's 3p — RESOLVED in Phase 49. The LP/3p trade-off is fundamental to asymmetric init.
Temperature annealing BREAKS the 3p ceiling (K=0.4148 > D=0.4018) but LP still short (0.4819 vs 0.4887) — RESOLVED in Phase 50. Pareto frontier shifted.
RESOLVED Phase 51: Static node=2.6 gets 3p=0.4001, annealed K gets 0.4148 — trajectory adds +0.015 bonus. Static is close but cannot match annealed.
Is there a theoretical ceiling for edge temp, or does the model keep wanting infinite sharpness?
Can DELTA-Full with targeted temperature match DELTA-Matched's compositional advantage?
Can DELTA-Full be compressed via importance pruning to match DELTA-Matched on 3p/5p?
Does curriculum compression during training converge to the same optimal capacity?
Why does DELTA peak at ep 125 while GraphGPS peaks at 150-200?
Would multi-seed 4p/5p confirm the accelerating advantage from Phase 44?
TEMPERATURE INVESTIGATION CLOSED (Phases 46-52). Landscape fully characterized. Three operating modes identified.
Multi-seed validation needed for K and N — are the 3p and 4p/5p advantages statistically robust across seeds?
ANSWERED Phase 53: NO — K's 3p and N's 4p/5p advantages are NOT robust. Multi-hop metrics are too noisy at 500 queries.
N needs full Phase 44 protocol (10k-query eval) to validate 4p/5p claims at publication quality (current results use 500-query cap)
Is the 500-query multi-hop eval fundamentally too noisy, or is the noise from model variance? Need to separate evaluation noise from training noise.
ANSWERED Phase 54: evaluation noise was dominant (66-84% reduction with 10kq). Model noise contributes std ≈ 0.003-0.017.
Can DELTA-Matched (157K, robust in Phase 45) be tested with temperature tuning? The smaller model showed robustness in multi-seed Phase 45.
DELTA-Full multi-hop investigation CLOSED. Next direction: Brain architecture — dynamic graph construction, adaptive pruning, constructor improvements.
Brain architecture produces precision/recall trade-off: constructed edges boost H@10 (+3.7%) but hurt H@1 (-1.4%) — constructor edges add broad coverage with some noise
BrainConstructor at 2% density generates exactly target edges (4870 ≈ 494²×0.02=4880) — density control works precisely
brain_hybrid still improving at epoch 150 (val_MRR monotonically increasing) — may need 200-300 epochs to converge — ANSWERED Phase 56: peaks at ep 150, overtrain after. 300 epochs hurts.
brain_hybrid training is 1.86× slower than delta_full (2468s vs 1329s) due to larger augmented graph
ANSWERED Phase 56: density=0.01 outperforms 0.02 by +0.012 MRR and +0.058 H@10 with half the edges. D (0.02) overfits faster. Lower density = higher quality construction.
Does density=0.005 continue the improvement trend, or is there a precision floor where too few edges provide insufficient coverage?
Can temperature annealing (Phase 50 K-style) be applied to brain_hybrid @ d=0.01 to push past 0.480 MRR?
brain_hybrid overfits by ep 150 — would lower lr, stronger sparsity weight, or explicit early stopping improve final MRR?
Constructor density is critical: d=0.01 (2435 edges) strictly dominates d=0.02 (4870 edges) on MRR (+0.012) and H@10 (+0.058) — restraint in edge construction improves quality
brain_hybrid @ d=0.01 matches delta_full MRR (0.4794 vs 0.4796) while smashing H@10 (+4.7%) — constructed edges add recall without precision penalty at optimal density
brain_hybrid overfits after ep 150: val_MRR drops 9.4% from peak over 150 epochs. D (d=0.02, 300ep) is WORSE than Phase 55 (d=0.02, 150ep) by -0.0095 MRR
Both densities produce exactly target edge count every epoch — BrainConstructor density control is precise and consistent
Sparsity loss is near-zero (0.0000) at both densities — constructor already produces sparse outputs without regularization pressure
Condition D peaks earlier (ep 120) than C (ep 150) — more edges cause faster overfitting, higher density hurts generalization

43.title: DropEdge robustness
43.result: CONFIRMED -- advantage structural at all 5 rates

45.title: Inference timing + multi-seed
45.result: CONFIRMED -- multi-seed robust, inference cost comparable

46.title: Attention sharpening via learnable temperature
46.result: PARTIAL -- dead heads reduced (full 83%->38%), edge/node temp divergence discovered, full 3p +0.029 but matched 3p -0.017

47.title: Layer-specific temperature initialization
47.result: PARTIAL -- B (L0 soft, L1+L2 sharp) achieves best LP MRR (0.4783) but 3p (0.3908) short of D (0.4018). Node attention requires sharpening to activate. Optimal node temp likely 2-3.

48.title: Asymmetric node/edge temperature
48.result: PARTIAL -- E (node=2,edge=6) achieves new LP MRR record (0.4856, +1.5%) and H@10 (0.8004). 3p gap persists (best 0.3970 vs D 0.4018). Node temps stable, edge always drifts UP. L0 temperature may explain D's 3p advantage.

49.title: L0 temperature + asymmetric L1+L2
49.result: PARTIAL -- H (L0=4,4 + E's L1+L2) achieves new LP MRR record (0.4887). 3p=0.3930 still below D (0.4018). L0 node temp irrelevant (L0 100% dead). LP/3p trade-off is fundamental to asymmetric temp init. I achieves best-ever H@10 (0.8076).

50.title: Temperature annealing (node temp schedule)
50.result: PARTIAL -- K (anneal 4→2 fast) achieves 3p=0.4148, FIRST to beat D's 0.4018 (+0.013). LP=0.4819 misses target (0.4856). M ties LP record (0.4887) but 3p=0.3803. L REJECTED. Pareto frontier shifted. Best checkpoint at node temp=2.6.

51.title: Static vs trajectory temperature optimization
51.result: PARTIAL -- P (anneal 4→2.5) achieves new LP MRR record (0.4890) and H@10 record (0.8014) but 3p=0.3823 FAIL. N (static 2.6) 3p=0.4001 just misses D's 0.4018 by 0.002. Trajectory confirmed: K (annealed)=0.4148 vs N (static)=0.4001 at same final temp=2.6, delta=+0.015. N has best-ever 4p (0.3426) and 5p (0.3788).

52.title: Closing K's LP gap via edge sharpness and faster annealing
52.result: PARTIAL -- Q (K+edge7) achieves NEW LP MRR record (0.4905) but 3p=0.3927 FAIL. S (K+edge7+faster) achieves NEW H@10 record (0.8045) but 3p=0.3789 FAIL. R (K+faster anneal) preserves 3p=0.4114 but LP=0.4793 FAIL. Edge init 7.0 boosts LP by +0.009 but hurts 3p by -0.022. Combined target still unachievable. TEMPERATURE INVESTIGATION CLOSED after 7 phases (46-52).

53.title: Multi-seed validation of K and N
53.result: CRITICAL REALITY CHECK -- K mean 3p=0.3699±0.0200 (BELOW baseline A=0.3725!). N mean 4p=0.2354±0.0618, 5p=0.2665±0.0738 (HUGE variance). LP MRR IS robust: K=0.4832±0.0052, N=0.4842±0.0089. Multi-hop eval at 500 queries is too noisy for single-seed conclusions. All temperature phase 3p/4p/5p claims are unreliable. Even seed=42 re-runs are non-reproducible (CUDA non-determinism).

54.title: High-power multi-hop evaluation (10k queries)
54.result: CONFIRMED variance hypothesis -- 10k queries reduce cross-seed std by 66-84% (avg K=75%, N=79%). Evaluation noise was dominant source of P53's variance. With tight CIs, K and N multi-hop are statistically indistinguishable. 500q vs 10kq absolute MRR differs dramatically (K 3p: 0.37→0.26) -- query distribution changes at scale. LP remains robust (K=0.4845±0.0051, N=0.4865±0.0086). Multi-hop investigation for DELTA-Full temperature tuning is CLOSED.

55.title: Brain Architecture Port — Differentiable Graph Construction
55.result: PARTIAL -- brain_hybrid MRR=0.4773 (misses 0.475 by -0.002) but H@10=0.7973 (+3.7% over delta_full). 4870 constructed edges improve recall at cost of precision (H@1 -1.4%). Architecture viable. Still improving at ep 150. Ran on RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell via Colab SSH.

56.title: Constructor Density Ablation — Precision vs Recall Trade-off
56.result: PARTIAL -- density=0.01 (C) outperforms density=0.02 (D): MRR 0.4794 vs 0.4678 (+0.012), H@10 0.8076 vs 0.7500 (+0.058), with half the edges (2435 vs 4870). Misses 0.480 MRR target by 0.0006. C matches delta_full MRR while preserving +4.7% H@10 advantage. Both conditions overfit after ep 150. Only C,D run (A,B deferred). Ran on RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell via Colab SSH + tmux.
